title: VPN Authentication Failures

compliance.assurance_rationale: PSD2 Art.97 Strong customer authentication — VPN authentication failures are used in PSP risk scoring to escalate SCA challenges.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of PSD2 Art.97 (Strong customer authentication) — Splunk UC-17.2.2: VPN Authentication Failures.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-17.2.2' running on VPN authentication logs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-17.2.2' running on VPN authentication logs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="authentication_failed"
| stats count by user, src
| where count > 5
| sort -count
description: Repeated VPN auth failures indicate credential attacks against the remote access perimeter, a primary attack vector.
value: Repeated VPN auth failures indicate credential attacks against the remote access perimeter, a primary attack vector.
implementation: Track VPN authentication failures by user and source IP. Alert on >5 failures per user per 15 minutes. Correlate with AD lockout events. Block source IPs with excessive failures. Report on attack patterns.
visualization: Table (failed auth events), Bar chart (failures by user), Geo map (source IPs), Line chart (failure rate trend).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: VPN authentication logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track VPN authentication failures by user and source IP. Alert on >5 failures per user per 15 minutes. Correlate with AD lockout events. Block source IPs with excessive failures. Report on attack patterns.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="authentication_failed"
| stats count by user, src
| where count > 5
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**VPN Authentication Failures** — Repeated VPN auth failures indicate credential attacks against the remote access perimeter, a primary attack vector.

Documented **Data sources**: VPN authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: cisco:asa. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="cisco:asa". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Access or firewall manager for the same users, rules, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed auth events), Bar chart (failures by user), Geo map (source IPs), Line chart (failure rate trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Cisco ASA 5506-X/5508-X/5516-X/5525-X/5545-X/5555-X, ASAv, Cisco Secure Firewall 3100/4200; Palo Alto PA-220/PA-440/PA-3200/PA-5200, GlobalProtect; Fortinet FortiGate 60F/100F/200F/600F/1800F, FortiGate SSL-VPN; Juniper SRX300/SRX1500/SRX4100/SRX4200, Junos Dynamic VPN
